page: Microsoft Azure E96s_v7 is an Esv7-family memory-optimized virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C x86_64 processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1D cache. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of RAM, providing a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory subsystem capabilities. The instance achieves top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, reaching 7543.56 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 349.82 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression reaches 604.05 GB/sec, and Redis SET operations perform at 160.72 million operations per second, both placing in the top tier. However, single-core CPU performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core scoring 2248.08, placing it in the average category. Large-model LLM inference text generation also shows average speeds, processing 4.66 tokens per second on a 70B model, whereas prompt processing for the same model is top-tier at 40.01 tokens per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the E96s_v7 offers high memory density per vCPU, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require local ephemeral storage. The primary tradeoff is the complete reliance on external Premium Storage for disk operations, which may introduce latency compared to local NVMe options. Given its high memory bandwidth, massive L3 cache, and strong multi-core execution, this server is designed for database operations, in-memory caching systems like Redis, large-scale data decompression, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E96s_v7 is an Esv7-family memory-optimized virtual machine powered by a 96-vCPU Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 768.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB per core, but lacks local storage and GPUs. Benchmarks show top-tier memory bandwidth, reaching 7543.56 GB/sec for cached reads, and strong multi-core performance, with Redis SET operations executing at 160.72 million operations per second. Single-core performance and large-model text generation speeds are average. The absence of local storage represents a key tradeoff, requiring external Premium Storage. This instance is optimized for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases, large-scale data decompression, and enterprise database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96s_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB of RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth, designed for database operations and in-memory caching without local storage.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring ninety-six dedicated vCPUs, high-bandwidth memory, and advanced vector extensions for enterprise database workloads.

Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 480 MB L3 cache and 4.2 GHz clock speed

No local storage, requiring external Premium Storage volumes
Suited for in-memory databases, Redis caching, and multi-threaded decompression
